title: ActiveSelectionEvent Object
description: This event fires whenever the contents of the active selection changes. This occurs as the user selects or unselects entities while using the Fusion Select command. The Select command is the default command that is always running if no other command is active. Pressing Escape terminates the currently active command and starts the Select command. If the Select command is running and you press Escape, it terminates the current Select command and starts a new one. This event is only associated with the selection associated with the Select command and does not fire when any other command is running. The event fires when there is any change to the active selection, including when the selection is cleared when the Select command is terminated. It is also fired when the user clicks in an open area of the canvas to clear the current selection.

methods.name: add
methods.description: Add a handler to be notified when the event occurs.

methods.name: classType
methods.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().

methods.name: remove
methods.description: Removes a handler from the event.

properties.name: isValid
properties.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

properties.name: name
properties.description: The name of the event - e.g. "DocumentOpening"

properties.name: objectType
properties.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object.   It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

properties.name: sender
properties.description: The object that is firing the event. For example, in the case of a command input event this will return the command.

add.title: ActiveSelectionEvent.add Method
add.description: Add a handler to be notified when the event occurs.
add.return_description: Returns true if the addition of the handler was successful.

parameters.name: handler
parameters.description: The handler object to be called when this event is fired.

classType.title: ActiveSelectionEvent.classType Method
classType.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().
classType.return_description: Returns a string indicating the type of the object.

remove.title: ActiveSelectionEvent.remove Method
remove.description: Removes a handler from the event.
remove.return_description: Returns true if removal of the handler was successful.

parameters.name: handler
parameters.description: The handler object to be removed from the event.

isValid.title: ActiveSelectionEvent.isValid Property
isValid.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = activeSelectionEvent_var.isValid

It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():
